steps.title: Identify the greatest common factor
steps.content: In $11x^{10}-11x^{9}+77x$, all coefficients (11, 11, and 77) share the factor 11. All terms contain at least one factor of $x$. The GCF is $11x$.

steps.title: Factor out 11x
steps.content:
When we factor out $11x$ from each term:
  • $11x^{10} = 11x \cdot x^{9}$
  • $11x^{9} = 11x \cdot x^{8}$
  • $77x = 11x \cdot 7$
Therefore: $11x^{10}-11x^{9}+77x = 11x(x^{9}-x^{8}+7)$. This matches choice D — D is correct.
